title: rungia subtilifolia
intro: Rungia subtilifolia, also known as the 'Indian Sage', is a plant species native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions like acne and eczema, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Rungia subtilifolia can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional medicine and modern herbal remedies.
